29.reason: [The scanf function expects the address of the variable to store the input, but st[len++] is the value at that index, not its address. It should be &st[len++].]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The scanf function expects the address of the variable to store the input, but st[len++] is the value at that index, not its address. It should be &st[len++].]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The scanf function expects the address of the variable to store the input, but st[len++] is the value at that index, not its address. It should be &st[len++].]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [The scanf function expects the address of the variable to store the input, but 'st[len++]' evaluates to the value at that index, not its address. It should be '&st[len++]'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [The scanf function expects the address of the variable to store the input, but 'st[len++]' is passed as a value (integer), not a pointer. This causes undefined behavior and likely a runtime crash or incorrect behavior. It should be '&st[len++]'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [The scanf function expects a pointer to the variable where the input will be stored, but 'st[len++]' is an integer value, not an address. It should be '&st[len++]'.]
